From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Set of horse races in Japan

For other uses, see [Classics (disambiguation)](/wiki/Classics_(disambiguation) "Classics (disambiguation)").

In [horse racing](/wiki/Horse_racing "Horse racing") in [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"), **The Classics** are a series of flat horse races. All of these races are organized by [Japan Racing Association](/wiki/Japan_Racing_Association "Japan Racing Association").[[1]](#cite_note-1) Each classic is run once each year and is restricted to intact horses that are three years old (i.e., excluding [geldings](/wiki/Gelding "Gelding")). Only the first five races are considered as classic whilst [Shuka Sho](/wiki/Shuka_Sho "Shuka Sho") were inaugurated in 1996 to replace [Queen Elizabeth II Cup](/wiki/Queen_Elizabeth_II_Cup_(Japan) "Queen Elizabeth II Cup (Japan)") as the third leg of triple tiara.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

| [Satsuki Sho](/wiki/Satsuki_Sho "Satsuki Sho") (Japanese 2,000 Guineas) | 2,000 meters | [Nakayama](/wiki/Nakayama_Racecourse "Nakayama Racecourse") | 1939 |
| [Yushun Himba](/wiki/Yushun_Himba "Yushun Himba") (Japanese Oaks) | May/June | 2,400 meters | [Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo_Racecourse "Tokyo Racecourse") | 1938 |
| [Tokyo Yushun](/wiki/Tokyo_Yushun "Tokyo Yushun") (Japanese Derby) | 2,400 meters | 1932 |
| [Kikuka Sho](/wiki/Kikuka-sh%C5%8D "Kikuka-shō") (Japanese St. Leger) | October | 3,000 meters | [Kyoto](/wiki/Kyoto_Racecourse "Kyoto Racecourse") | 1938 |
| [Shūka Sho](/wiki/Sh%C5%ABka_Sho "Shūka Sho") | 2,000 meters | 1996 |

## Triple crown for fillies (Triple Tiara)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Japanese_Classic_Races&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Triple crown for fillies (Triple Tiara)")]

Japanese fillies who have won the Triple Crown title by winning the [Oka Sho](/wiki/Oka_Sho "Oka Sho"), [Japan Oaks](/wiki/Yushun_Himba "Yushun Himba") and one of the following: [Shuka Sho](/wiki/Shuka_Sho "Shuka Sho") (1996 onwards), or the [Queen Elizabeth II Cup](/wiki/Queen_Elizabeth_II_Cup_(Japan) "Queen Elizabeth II Cup (Japan)") (formerly the [Victoria Cup](/w/index.php?title=Victoria_Cup_(Japan)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Victoria Cup (Japan) (page does not exist)"), from 1970 to 1975; not a Classics race, but was counted as the third leg of the Triple Tiara before 1996).[[12]](#cite_note-12) Before the establishment of these crowns in 1970, the third leg of the triple tiara used to be [Kikuka Sho](/wiki/Kikuka_Sho "Kikuka Sho") which is the basis for the original triple tiara in England (1000 Guineas, Epsom Oaks and St. Leger Stakes) as the final race is open for both colts and fillies. [Kurifuji](/wiki/Kurifuji "Kurifuji") gained an uncommon status as both "double crown" and "double tiara" horse as she won [Tokyo Yushun](/wiki/Tokyo_Yushun "Tokyo Yushun"), Yushun Himba and Kikuka Sho back in 1943.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

## Triple Crown for Dirt races

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Japanese_Classic_Races&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Triple Crown for Dirt races")]

Recently established beginning with the 2024 season, no horse has won the Dirt triple crown. The races included in this circuit are [Haneda Hai](/wiki/Haneda_Hai "Haneda Hai"), [Tokyo Derby](/wiki/Tokyo_Derby_(horse_race) "Tokyo Derby (horse race)"), and [Japan Dirt Classic](/wiki/Japan_Dirt_Classic "Japan Dirt Classic").
